topics.title: Python Decorators
topics.summary: Functions that wrap other functions to extend or modify their behavior without changing their source code.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to return the wrapper function
Forgetting to use functools.wraps, losing the original function's metadata
Confusing decorators with plain function calls
topics.practice_idea: Create a decorator that logs the name and execution time of any function it wraps.

topics.title: Python List Comprehensions
topics.summary: A concise syntax to build lists (and other collections) by transforming or filtering an iterable in a single expression.
key_concepts: Expression + for clause + optional if clause
common_mistakes: Writing comprehensions so nested they become unreadable
Using a comprehension purely for side effects instead of a for loop
Forgetting that the loop variable leaks into it only in Python 2, causing confusion in Python 3
topics.practice_idea: Rewrite a for loop that builds a filtered, transformed list as a single list comprehension.

topics.title: JavaScript Closures
topics.summary: A closure is a function that remembers the variables from the scope it was created in, even after that scope has finished executing.
common_mistakes: Creating closures inside loops without understanding variable capture (classic var vs let issue)
Assuming closures copy variables instead of referencing them
Causing memory leaks by holding unnecessary references alive
topics.practice_idea: Write a counter() function that returns an increment function keeping its own private count.

topics.title: Flask Routing and Views
topics.summary: Flask maps URL patterns to Python functions (views) that generate the HTTP response for that route.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to specify methods=['POST'] for form submissions
Mixing business logic directly inside view functions instead of separating concerns
topics.practice_idea: Build a small Flask app with a dynamic route /topics/<topic_id> that renders a Jinja2 template.

topics.title: MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline
topics.summary: A framework for processing documents through a sequence of stages (filter, group, sort, reshape) to produce computed results.
common_mistakes: Placing $match too late in the pipeline, hurting performance
Forgetting that stage order changes the result
Overusing aggregation for tasks a simple query could handle
topics.practice_idea: Write an aggregation pipeline that groups documents by category and returns the average of a numeric field per group.

topics.title: Python WebSockets with async/await
topics.summary: WebSockets provide a persistent, full-duplex connection between client and server, useful for real-time communication.
common_mistakes: Blocking the event loop with synchronous code inside an async handler
Forgetting to await coroutines, causing silent bugs
topics.practice_idea: Build a minimal chat server using the websockets library that broadcasts any received message to all connected clients.
